messages.author: Bobabate
messages.text: Perhaps a first in the Toronto? We now have two router/rooms that can talk to each other downtown Toronto. Davisville to Bickford. Also now have two other people that can reach at least one of these radios.
messages.content_html: Perhaps a first in the Toronto? We now have two router/rooms that can talk to each other downtown Toronto. Davisville to Bickford. Also now have two other people that can reach at least one of these radios.
messages.reactions_html: 2

messages.author: mutemule
messages.text: Excellent! I’ve left Toronto proper, and I think that might be my node in between the two. I need to submit a manual removal request, my own attempts don’t seem to work.
messages.content_html: Excellent! I’ve left Toronto proper, and I think that might be my node in between the two. I need to submit a manual removal request, my own attempts don’t seem to work.

messages.author: Bobabate
messages.text: Anyone interested please reach out. Trying to build some solid connections to downtown Toronto.
messages.content_html: Anyone interested please reach out. Trying to build some solid connections to downtown Toronto.

messages.author: Ernie
messages.text: What settings are people using? I find the Alternate settings provide the best range.
messages.content_html: What settings are people using? I find the Alternate settings provide the best range.

messages.author: Bobabate
messages.text: Hey! I’m using the new recommended setting. As are any of the repeaters that can sort of reach each other in Toronto. Apparently it avoids our power meters interfering with it. What’s your location?
messages.content_html: Hey! I’m using the new recommended setting. As are any of the repeaters that can sort of reach each other in Toronto. Apparently it avoids our power meters interfering with it. What’s your location?

messages.author: Ernie
messages.text: What's the new recommend settings? SF 11 and BW 250?
messages.content_html: What's the new recommend settings? SF 11 and BW 250?

messages.author: Ernie
messages.text: How’s the Meshcore weekend conversation going?
messages.content_html: How’s the Meshcore weekend conversation going?

messages.author: Bobabate
messages.text: Good. Someone came online in reach of my repeater. Messages zooming back and forth easily. Saw someone else drop a node on the Map but they are using European radio settings so not sure what will happen there. It’s hard to reach people on Meshtastic to tell them. And there are a few people that wont even consider it. Which I find so weird. I guess they like half conversations and looking at the pretty map of fiction. The big barrier of Meshcore is needing more than one device. Adding repeaters is so important. But once you use MeshCore it’s hard to take Meshtastic seriously. I’ve reduced my MT footprint down to one node just to keep an eye on it. Everything else is now running MeshCore.
messages.content_html: Good. Someone came online in reach of my repeater. Messages zooming back and forth easily. Saw someone else drop a node on the Map but they are using European radio settings so not sure what will happen there. It’s hard to reach people on Meshtastic to tell them. And there are a few people that wont even consider it. Which I find so weird. I guess they like half conversations and looking at the pretty map of fiction. The big barrier of Meshcore is needing more than one device. Adding repeaters is so important. But once you use MeshCore it’s hard to take Meshtastic seriously. I’ve reduced my MT footprint down to one node just to keep an eye on it. Everything else is now running MeshCore.

messages.author: Bobabate
messages.text: Make sure you have set the radio to the Canada/US setting. Should be plenty of radios around in the west end. I’m there! Can take a few hours to start to hear Repeater Adverts. Send out a few adverts and a few messages in public. If anyone hears you they will respond. Also try sending “!help” in public.

I'm going to try going for a walk around the neighborhood, maybe by the lake and/or near confirmed nodes on the map websites.

messages.author: Obsessive Audio
messages.text: @Bobabate I got a T-Deck Plus, which is a way better experience for me than the T-Echo, the first purchase. I'm picking up many repeaters.. Which makes me think, what's the difference between this and the T-Echo which picked up close to nothing except local? I mean in my home spot, which I thought was dead, the T-Deck is working. The T-Deck was the first device for me to pick up something from the actual mesh. (edited)

messages.author: Obsessive Audio
messages.text: Any recommendations for repeaters? Permanent solar install on the roof, gotta call Doctor Antenna for installation. (edited)
messages.content_html: Any recommendations for repeaters? Permanent solar install on the roof, gotta call Doctor Antenna for installation. (edited)

messages.author: Bobabate
messages.text: For off the shelf no hassle repeaters either the Rak Mini Repeater or the Seeed P1 Pro. Both have done well in the Canadian winter. You may want to upgrade the antennas but you don’t need to. The Alpha is super popular and easy to add to the P1. Davisville is this config. (edited)
messages.content_html: For off the shelf no hassle repeaters either the Rak Mini Repeater or the Seeed P1 Pro. Both have done well in the Canadian winter. You may want to upgrade the antennas but you don’t need to. The Alpha is super popular and easy to add to the P1. Davisville is this config. (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 1
messages.reply_html:
Obsessive Audio
Any recommendations for repeaters? Permanent solar install on the roof, gotta call Doctor Antenna for installation. (edited)
